evals.expected_output: Activates robots: reads issue ABC-12's acceptance criteria and the assembler repo contract, creates one branch/worktree whose name carries the Linear issue id, implements only the acceptance criteria citing bus-first (and tdd/diagnose as needed), runs the contract's targeted checks, prepares a review packet, and opens a PR — preserving one-issue-one-branch-one-PR and not silently closing the issue (closeout is rocket-launch).

evals.prompt: Continue work on the offline-mode sync issue.
evals.expected_output: Activates robots: resumes the existing one-issue-one-branch-one-PR for that Linear issue, keeps the issue-id branch intact for the bridge, picks up the remaining acceptance criteria, runs targeted checks, updates the review packet/PR, and does not silently close the issue.

Despite the typo, activates robots and interprets it as starting end-to-end work on issue ABC-12: contract + acceptance criteria, one issue-id branch/worktree, minimal implementation, targeted checks, review packet, and a PR — without owning closeout.

evals.prompt: Triage the new bugs and sort what's ready.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT activate robots; routes to dispatch for classification, prioritization, and routing of incoming work. robots only builds an already-tracked, ready issue and does not triage.

evals.prompt: The offline-mode PR is ready — review the gate, merge it, and close the issue.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT activate robots for closeout; routes to rocket-launch to run the review/merge gate and let the bridge close the Linear issue. robots prepares the PR review-ready but never owns merge or issue closure.
